sn39.1-15:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā sāriputto vajjīsu viharati ukkacelāyaṁ gaṅgāya nadiyā tīre.
sn39.1-15:1.1.eng: At one time Venerable Sāriputta was staying in the land of the Vajjis near Ukkacelā on the bank of the Ganges river.

sn39.1-15:1.2.pli: Atha kho sāmaṇḍako paribbājako yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
sn39.1-15:1.2.eng: Then the wanderer Sāmaṇḍaka went up to Venerable Sāriputta and exchanged greetings with him.

sn39.1-15:1.3.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho sāmaṇḍako paribbājako āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
sn39.1-15:1.3.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Sāriputta:

sn39.1-15:2.1.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘extinguishment’.

sn39.1-15:2.3.eng: “Reverend, the ending of greed, hate, and delusion

sn39.1-15:2.5.pli: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etassa nibbānassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti?
sn39.1-15:2.5.eng: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for realizing this extinguishment?”

sn39.1-15:2.6.pli: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etassa nibbānassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.

sn39.1-15:3.1.pli: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā etassa nibbānassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti?

sn39.1-15:3.2.pli: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo, etassa nibbānassa sacchikiriyāya, seyyathidaṁ—
sn39.1-15:3.2.eng: “It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:

sn39.1-15:3.3.eng: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.

sn39.1-15:3.4.pli: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā, etassa nibbānassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.
sn39.1-15:3.4.eng: This is the path, the practice, for realizing this extinguishment.”

sn39.1-15:3.5.pli: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etassa nibbānassa sacchikiriyāya.
sn39.1-15:3.5.eng: “Reverend, this is a fine path, a fine practice, for realizing this extinguishment.

sn39.1-15:3.6.eng: Just this much is enough to be diligent.”

sn39.1-15:4.1.eng: (Tell in full  as the Linked Discourses with Jambukhādaka.)

sn39.16:1.1.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, imasmiṁ dhammavinaye dukkaran”ti?
sn39.16:1.1.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, in this teaching and training, what is hard to do?”

sn39.16:1.2.eng: “Going forth, reverend, is hard to do in this teaching and training.”

sn39.16:1.3.eng: “But what’s hard to do for someone who has gone forth?”

sn39.16:1.4.eng: “When you’ve gone forth it’s hard to be satisfied.”

sn39.16:1.5.eng: “But what’s hard to do for someone who is satisfied?”

sn39.16:1.6.eng: “When you’re satisfied, it’s hard to practice in line with the teaching.”

sn39.16:1.7.eng: “But if a mendicant practices  in line with the teaching, will it take them long to become a perfected one?”

sn39.16:1.11.eng: The Linked Discourses with Sāmaṇḍaka are complete.

sn40.1:0.3.eng: A Question About the First Absorption

sn40.1:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
sn40.1:1.1.eng: At one time Venerable Mahāmoggallāna was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.

sn40.1:1.2.pli: Tatra kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhikkhū āmantesi:
sn40.1:1.2.eng: There Venerable Mahāmoggallāna addressed the mendicants:

sn40.1:1.4.pli: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa paccassosuṁ.

sn40.1:2.1.pli: “Idha mayhaṁ, āvuso, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
sn40.1:2.1.eng: “Just now, reverends, as I was in private retreat this thought came to mind:

sn40.1:2.2.eng: ‘They speak of this thing called the “first absorption”.

sn40.1:2.5.pli: ‘idha bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
sn40.1:2.5.eng: ‘It’s when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.

sn40.1:2.6.eng: This is called the first absorption.’

sn40.1:2.7.pli: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharāmi.
sn40.1:2.7.eng: And so … I was entering and remaining in the first absorption.

sn40.1:2.8.pli: Tassa mayhaṁ, āvuso, iminā vihārena viharato kāmasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti.
sn40.1:2.8.eng: While I was in that meditation, perception and focus accompanied by sensual pleasures beset me.

sn40.1:3.1.pli: Atha kho maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā iddhiyā upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca:
sn40.1:3.1.eng: Then the Buddha came up to me with his psychic power and said,

sn40.1:3.3.pli: Mā, brāhmaṇa, paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ pamādo, paṭhame jhāne cittaṁ saṇṭhapehi, paṭhame jhāne cittaṁ ekodiṁ karohi, paṭhame jhāne cittaṁ samādahā’ti.
sn40.1:3.3.eng: Don’t neglect the first absorption, brahmin! Settle your mind in the first absorption; unify your mind and immerse it in the first absorption.’

sn40.1:3.4.pli: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, aparena samayena vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihāsiṁ.
sn40.1:3.4.eng: And so, after some time … I entered and remained in the first absorption.

sn40.1:3.5.pli: Yañhi taṁ, āvuso, sammā vadamāno vadeyya:
sn40.1:3.5.eng: So if anyone should be rightly called

sn40.1:3.6.pli: ‘satthārānuggahito sāvako mahābhiññataṁ patto’ti, mamaṁ taṁ sammā vadamāno vadeyya:
sn40.1:3.6.eng: a disciple who attained to great direct knowledge with help from the Teacher, it’s me.”

sn40.10:1.1.pli: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evameva—jetavane antarahito devesu tāvatiṁsesu pāturahosi.
sn40.10:1.1.eng: And then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, vanished from Jeta’s Grove and reappeared among the gods of the thirty-three.

sn40.10:1.2.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo pañcahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:1.2.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:

sn40.10:2.1.eng: “Lord of gods, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha.

sn40.10:2.2.pli: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:2.2.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:2.3.eng: It’s good to go for refuge to the teaching.

sn40.10:2.4.pli: Dhammasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:2.4.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:2.5.eng: It’s good to go for refuge to the Saṅgha.

sn40.10:2.6.pli: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
sn40.10:2.6.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”

sn40.10:3.1.eng: “My good Moggallāna, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha …

sn40.10:3.2.pli: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:3.3.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhammasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti.

sn40.10:3.4.pli: Dhammasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:3.6.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”

sn40.10:4.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo chahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:4.1.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with six hundred deities …

sn40.10:4.2.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo sattahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:4.2.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with seven hundred deities …

sn40.10:4.3.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo aṭṭhahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:4.3.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eight hundred deities …

sn40.10:4.4.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo asītiyā devatāsahassehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:4.4.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eighty thousand deities …

sn40.10:5.2.pli: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:5.4.pli: Dhammasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:5.6.pli: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.

sn40.10:6.2.pli: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:6.3.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhammasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti …pe…

sn40.10:6.4.pli: sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti.

sn40.10:6.5.pli: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.

sn40.10:7.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo pañcahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:7.1.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:

sn40.10:8.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, devānaminda, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:8.1.eng: “Lord of gods, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha:

sn40.10:8.2.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
sn40.10:8.2.eng: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’

sn40.10:8.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:8.3.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:9.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:9.1.eng: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the teaching:

sn40.10:9.2.pli: ‘svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo sandiṭṭhiko akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.
sn40.10:9.2.eng: ‘The teaching is well explained by the Buddha—apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.’

sn40.10:9.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:9.3.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:10.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:10.1.eng: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the Saṅgha:

sn40.10:10.2.pli: ‘suppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ujuppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ñāyappaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, sāmīcippaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, yadidaṁ cattāri purisayugāni aṭṭha purisapuggalā esa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā’ti.
sn40.10:10.2.eng: ‘The Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples is practicing the way that’s good, direct, systematic, and proper. It consists of the four pairs, the eight individual persons. This is the Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples that is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.’

sn40.10:10.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:10.3.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:11.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi acchiddehi asabalehi akammāsehi bhujissehi viññuppasatthehi aparāmaṭṭhehi samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
sn40.10:11.1.eng: It’s good to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones, unbroken, impeccable, spotless, and unmarred, liberating, praised by sensible people, not mistaken, and leading to immersion.

sn40.10:11.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
sn40.10:11.2.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”

sn40.10:12.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:12.1.eng: “My good Moggallāna, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha …

sn40.10:12.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:13.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:13.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:14.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:14.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:15.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
sn40.10:15.1.eng: and to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones …

sn40.10:15.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
sn40.10:15.2.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”

sn40.10:16.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo chahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe….
sn40.10:16.1.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with six hundred deities …

sn40.10:16.2.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo sattahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe….
sn40.10:16.2.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with seven hundred deities …

sn40.10:16.3.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo aṭṭhahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe….
sn40.10:16.3.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eight hundred deities …

sn40.10:16.4.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo asītiyā devatāsahassehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:16.4.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eighty thousand deities …

sn40.10:17.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, devānaminda, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:17.2.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.

sn40.10:17.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:18.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:18.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:19.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:19.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:20.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.

sn40.10:20.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.

sn40.10:21.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:21.2.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.

sn40.10:21.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:22.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:22.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:23.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:23.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:24.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.

sn40.10:24.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.

sn40.10:25.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo pañcahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami …pe…
sn40.10:25.1.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna,

sn40.10:25.2.pli: ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:25.2.eng: bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:

sn40.10:26.1.eng: “Lord of gods, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha.

sn40.10:26.2.pli: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:26.2.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:26.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
sn40.10:26.3.eng: They surpass other gods in ten respects:

sn40.10:26.4.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehi.
sn40.10:26.4.eng: heavenly lifespan, beauty, happiness, glory, sovereignty, sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.

sn40.10:27.1.eng: It’s good to go for refuge to the teaching …

sn40.10:27.2.pli: Dhammasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:27.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:27.4.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehi.

sn40.10:28.1.eng: It’s good to go for refuge to the Saṅgha.

sn40.10:28.2.pli: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:28.2.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:28.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
sn40.10:28.3.eng: They surpass other gods in ten respects:

sn40.10:28.4.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.
sn40.10:28.4.eng: heavenly lifespan, beauty, happiness, glory, sovereignty, sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.”

sn40.10:29.1.eng: “My good Moggallāna, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha …”

sn40.10:29.2.pli: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:29.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:30.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhammasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti …pe….

sn40.10:31.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti.

sn40.10:31.2.pli: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:31.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:31.4.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.

sn40.10:32.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo chahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:32.1.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with six hundred deities …

sn40.10:32.2.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo sattahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:32.2.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with seven hundred deities …

sn40.10:32.3.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo aṭṭhahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:32.3.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eight hundred deities …

sn40.10:32.4.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo asītiyā devatāsahassehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:32.4.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eighty thousand deities …

sn40.10:33.2.pli: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:33.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:35.2.pli: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:35.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:35.4.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.

sn40.10:36.2.pli: sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhammasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti …pe…

sn40.10:36.3.pli: sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti.

sn40.10:36.4.pli: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:36.5.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:36.6.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.

sn40.10:37.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo pañcahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:37.1.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:

sn40.10:38.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, devānaminda, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:38.1.eng: “Lord of gods, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha:

sn40.10:38.2.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
sn40.10:38.2.eng: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha … teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’

sn40.10:38.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
sn40.10:38.3.eng: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

sn40.10:38.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
sn40.10:38.4.eng: They surpass other gods in ten respects:

sn40.10:38.5.eng: heavenly lifespan, beauty, happiness, glory, sovereignty, sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.

sn40.10:39.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:39.1.eng: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the teaching …

sn40.10:39.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti …pe….

sn40.10:40.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:40.1.eng: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the Saṅgha …

sn40.10:40.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti …pe….

sn40.10:41.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
sn40.10:41.1.eng: It’s good to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones …”

sn40.10:41.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:41.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:42.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
sn40.10:42.1.eng: “My good Moggallāna, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha …”

sn40.10:42.2.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.

sn40.10:42.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:42.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:43.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:43.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:43.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:44.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:44.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti …pe….

sn40.10:45.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.

sn40.10:45.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:45.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:46.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo chahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:46.1.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with six hundred deities …

sn40.10:46.2.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo sattahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:46.2.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with seven hundred deities …

sn40.10:46.3.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo aṭṭhahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
sn40.10:46.3.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eight hundred deities …

sn40.10:46.4.pli: atha kho sakko devānamindo asītiyā devatāsahassehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
sn40.10:46.4.eng: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eighty thousand deities …

sn40.10:47.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, devānaminda, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:47.2.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.

sn40.10:47.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:47.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:47.5.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehi.

sn40.10:48.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:48.2.pli: ‘svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo sandiṭṭhiko akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.

sn40.10:48.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:48.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:49.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:49.2.pli: ‘suppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ujuppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ñāyappaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, sāmīcippaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, yadidaṁ cattāri purisayugāni aṭṭha purisapuggalā esa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā’ti.

sn40.10:49.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:49.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:50.1.pli: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi acchiddehi asabalehi akammāsehi bhujissehi viññuppasatthehi aparāmaṭṭhehi samādhisaṁvattanikehi.

sn40.10:50.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:50.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:50.4.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.

sn40.10:51.1.pli: “Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:51.2.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.

sn40.10:51.3.pli: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:51.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:52.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:52.3.pli: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:52.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:53.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:

sn40.10:53.3.pli: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:53.4.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:54.1.pli: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.

sn40.10:54.2.pli: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

sn40.10:54.3.pli: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—

sn40.10:54.4.pli: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.
